Details: Based on bench-scale studies, biological treatment for cylindrospermopsin removal shows a wide range of effectiveness values from 0 to greater than 99 percent removal. Treatment with biological agents alone seems to be ineffective to marginally effective (less than 0 to 24 percent) [2746, 2766]. Removal rates of greater than 90 percent were observed when biological agents (manganese-oxidizing bacteria) were pared with chemical additives (manganese, iron, and others) to stimulate bacterial oxidation of cyanotoxins. The results suggest that oxidation of manganese is pivotal for effective removal [2766]. These studies did not address practical implementation of the biological process within a drinking water treatment plant (e.g., bioreactor design and operating parameters).
